title: Xtra Crisp Indian Dasima Kombu Kachori
description: A crispy and adventurous Indian dish featuring dasima-kombu, this kachori is a perfect blend of flavors and textures, made with a satvik-friendly approach and a brine-wet method.

ingredients.name: Dasima kombu
ingredients.notes: dried and cut into small pieces

instructions: Soak the dasima kombu pieces in water for at least 4 hours to make them soft and pliable.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the all purpose flour, ghee, salt, cumin seeds, coriander powder, and red chili powder.
Add the soaked dasima kombu pieces to the flour mixture and mix well.
Gradually add the water to the mixture and knead until a soft dough forms.
Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into small balls and flatten each ball into a thin circle.
Heat the brine solution in a large pan over medium heat and add the flattened dough circles to it.
Cook the kachoris for 5-7 minutes on each side, until they are crispy and golden brown.
Remove the kachoris from the brine solution and let them cool on a paper towel.
Serve the xtra crisp Indian dasima kombu kachoris hot and enjoy.
